ticket.subject: PluginHive Shopify Ship, Rate and Track for FedEx App On-boarding call for store cooks-gf-sourdough.myshopify.com
ticket.raw_subject: PluginHive Shopify Ship, Rate and Track for FedEx App On-boarding call for store cooks-gf-sourdough.myshopify.com
ticket.description:
Hello Ron,

Greeting from Shopify Partner PluginHive!

Thank you for installing our Shopify App - **PH Ship Rate & Track for FedEx by PluginHive** on **cooks-gf-sourdough.myshopify.com**

Our customer on-boarding team will contact you soon. This call is intended to help you complete the shipping configuration on your store. Please confirm if the below mentioned contact details are correct. If not, kindly provide an alternative contact details to get in touch with you.

Contact Number: | Email Address: ronlari@gmail.com

You can also refer our documentation to set up the app and start shipping - click here to know more

Thanks and Regards,
Team PluginHive
https://www.pluginhive.com/about/#team

comments.body:
Hello Ron,

Good day!

This is Sibi, from the PH Ship, Rate & Track for FedEx App. Thank you for installing our solution.

We’d be happy to schedule a **free onboarding call** over Zoom screen-share to assist you with setting up and configuring the app as per your needs.

Please respond to this email so we can arrange a convenient time for the call. (or) You can also book a slot for the call according to your availability using this link - **[****Click Here****]**

Thank you, and we look forward to your response.

Best regards,
Sibi
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
**Call Summary:**
Customer Ron is a developer who builds Shopify stores and transfers them to clients based on requirements, including configuring shipping before going live. He mentioned that they handle multiple projects and, if this setup works well, they may bring in more customers.

**Current Use Case: All is possible with MCSL**

- Connect both FedEx and UPS carriers
- Enable both One Rate and Ground services
- Pack items into the most suitable boxes based on weight and dimensions
- Display specific services based on zones (e.g., only Ground and 2-Day for California, and 2-Day & Overnight for other states)
- Show the cheapest service across carriers (e.g., compare UPS Ground and FedEx Ground, and display the lowest as “Ground Shipping” by renaming services)
- Shopify UPS rates are not feasible; informed that EasyPost UPS provides competitive rates, and the customer is interested in trying this

**Pending Action:**
The customer has installed the app but is unable to accept charges since billing is not yet set up on the store.
The customer would like to:

- Test the app and complete the setup as per client requirements
- Move to live later and then set up payments to accept charges

**Request:**
Can we allow the customer to install and use the app on a transferred client store without accepting charges?
**Additional Note:**
The customer mentioned they are able to use the FedEx app without accepting charges, but are unable to proceed with our app.

**Store Details:**
Store URL – cooks-gf-sourdough.myshopify.com
Registered Email –ronlari@gmail.com

Customer expecting to go live by Monday, so they are expecting the results by today or max tomorrow

Call recording -
comments.html_body:
Call Summary:
Customer Ron is a developer who builds Shopify stores and transfers them to clients based on requirements, including configuring shipping before going live. He mentioned that they handle multiple projects and, if this setup works well, they may bring in more customers.
 
Current Use Case: All is possible with MCSL
  • Connect both FedEx and UPS carriers
  • Enable both One Rate and Ground services
  • Pack items into the most suitable boxes based on weight and dimensions
  • Display specific services based on zones (e.g., only Ground and 2-Day for California, and 2-Day & Overnight for other states)
  • Show the cheapest service across carriers (e.g., compare UPS Ground and FedEx Ground, and display the lowest as “Ground Shipping” by renaming services)
  • Shopify UPS rates are not feasible; informed that EasyPost UPS provides competitive rates, and the customer is interested in trying this
 
Pending Action:
The customer has installed the app but is unable to accept charges since billing is not yet set up on the store.
The customer would like to:
  • Test the app and complete the setup as per client requirements
  • Move to live later and then set up payments to accept charges
 
Request:
Can we allow the customer to install and use the app on a transferred client store without accepting charges?
Additional Note:
The customer mentioned they are able to use the FedEx app without accepting charges, but are unable to proceed with our app.
 
Store Details:
Store URL – cooks-gf-sourdough.myshopify.com 
Registered Email –ronlari@gmail.com
 
Customer expecting to go live by Monday, so they are expecting the results by today or max tomorrow
 
Call recording -

Thank you for your time on the call.

As discussed, could you please confirm your availability for tomorrow?

Additionally, please ensure that all product weights are updated in Shopify before the call. It would also be helpful if you could add all your packaging boxes under the packaging settings.

Looking forward to your response.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
**Call Summary:**
We assisted the customer with the FedEx account integration. We also reviewed product and packaging configurations, updated boxes and one product, and tested multiple order combinations. The rates are now working as expected.

The customer has a specific requirement: they do not want FedEx One Rate to be displayed when items are packed into custom boxes. They prefer One Rate only for FedEx Express boxes. This requires L3 team assistance to enable the appropriate toggle.

Additionally, we registered an EasyPost account during the call. However, the API Key section was not visible. A support request has been raised with EasyPost, and we expect this to be resolved by tomorrow.

**Next Steps (Upcoming Call):**

- Connect the EasyPost account
- Configure rules to display UPS rates along with FedEx
The customer was happy with the progress and is excited to see the final setup.

**L3 Cases:**

1. The customer does not want FedEx One Rate for custom boxes and needs it only for FedEx Express boxes.
Requesting L3 team to activate the “MCSL FedEx REST – Fetch Negotiated Rate” toggle for this customer (already posted in the channel).

2. MCSL app partner accessibility issue:
We have around 30 installs in the last 30 days, mostly from developer stores. A developer reported they could install the FedEx app but not the MCSL app.
Requesting the team to check this, including verification with the Australia Post app.
Slack thread - https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1776970538730449?thread_ts=1776964015.996819&cid=C02E15X8X0C

Call recording - https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1q-nkXoo_9DxH2JXTAqIkT78w795jB5V2?usp=sharing

comments.body:
Hello Ron,

Thank you for your reply.

To use UPS services, you either need to connect your UPS account or use an EasyPost UPS account. I'm afraid there is no other service.

We appreciate our understanding.

Feel free to reachout to us anytime you need assistance from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive
